code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Immediate failure (<1 month) or safety hazards
    - Explicit warnings: "dangerous," "returned," "don't buy"
    - Final negative updates override initial positives

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality failure despite partial utility
    - Multiple flaws affecting daily use
    - Regret language: "waste of money," "false advertising"

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with conditional utility
    - "Better than nothing" assessments
    - Requires significant user effort for basic function

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Explicit star deduction without strong endorsement
    - Minor drawbacks in non-core aspects
    - Example: "Deduct 1 star for X but still functional"

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - "Still 5 stars" declarations despite any criticism
    - Multi-feature praise outweighing single drawback
    - Transformative benefits: "exceeded expectations," "life-changing"
    - Price praise + "worth every penny" phrasing

    EDGE CASE HANDLING:
    - Deduction + recommendation →5 ("Deduct 1 star but love")
    - Aspect scores < overall rating →5 ("4 for comfort but 5 overall")
    - Safety issues →1 regardless of other positives
    - Final sentiment dominates (prioritize last paragraph)
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall - amazing features make this exceptional!"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating override despite aspect critique. Multi-feature praise confirms 5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "UPDATE: Lasted 2 months before breaking. Not worth repairing."
<Reasoning>Final negative update overrides initial positives. Short lifespan →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Solves scraping needs perfectly! Dishwasher unsafe but still 5 stars for effectiveness."
<Reasoning>Explicit 5-star declaration overrides convenience drawback.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "The towels are soft but shed constantly. Deducting one star for shedding issue."
<Reasoning>Functional deficiency without mitigating endorsement. Explicit deduction →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5 (New Error Coverage):
Review: "I like this Turkish cotton towel but deduct one star for non-dishwasher safety."
<Reasoning>Explicit deduction without strong recommendation. Core functionality intact →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6 (New Borderline):
Review: "Blender looks stunning and makes decent smoothies. Expected better performance."
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise can't compensate for adequate functionality. Mixed →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Assembly drew blood! Sharp edges everywhere. Returned immediately."
<Reasoning>Safety hazard overrides all considerations. Clear 1-star.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8 (Error Prevention):
Review: "Works well but not dishwasher safe. Still recommend for effectiveness!"
<Reasoning>Recommendation overrides convenience issue. Functional excellence →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 8
  gold 4 -> 3: 4
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "We are both tough on dish towels and use them constantly. Not only do we cook 90% of our meals at home but we also use fresh fruits and veggies with pretty much every meal and that means a lot of washing, drying and carrying things around...why carry..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I bought these mainly for sending leftovers. It is easier than sending my rubbermaid containers because I am not out much money if they don't come back."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [4, 3, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I like the Stasher Snack bag. It is the perfect size for snacks on the go. I also like that you can store, cook, put in the microwave or even in the dishwasher! This is great for small treats. Don't try to overstuff. You don't want the seal to get fo..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "This really is a beautiful mug. It's so beautiful I am thinking of just using it as decor rather than actually using it. It's not really big enough to use for my morning cup of coffee (I need a BIG mug for that), although it's the perfect size for my..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Over the past two months or so I've gotten numerous sets of kitchen towels and the process was always the same. Try them. See the weren't absorbent. Wash them numerous times with fabric softener to make them somewhat absorbent and keep at least four ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "I always hate not being able to get the last remains out of a jar or a can. This definitely takes care of that problem and with ease. Pour out what you can and simply use this to scrape the rest out. The reason I'm deducting a star is because it isn'..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "EDIT: A year of use later it's still humming along and doing its job just fine. Sometimes it seems like it doesn't pick up quickly that it should be upping its speed on its own in the auto setting, but maybe it's just taking longer for the air to rea..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 17 borderline out of 80 examples."}
